title: Boat Rental in Tarragona: A Practical 2026 Guide to Choosing Your Vessel
html:
Tarragona's marina-lined coast offers a prime base for Mediterranean boating, with daily charter rates ranging from €300 for a small motorboat to over €2,500 for a crewed yacht. Whether you want to explore the Costa Daurada or anchor off a quiet cove, you can compare verified listings on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace for renting boats.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Tarragona?

Prices vary by boat type and season, with half-day rentals from €300 and full-day crewed charters from €1,200. The peak season of July and August commands higher rates, while May, June and September offer better value. For a guided overview of options, book a charter online through BoatHire24 and compare prices across multiple operators.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Tarragona?

You can rent small motorboats (under 15 hp) without a licence, but larger vessels usually require a Spanish PER qualification or an equivalent ICC. Always confirm with the operator, who will advise on accepted licences and may offer a skipper for unlicensed guests. The team at BoatHire24 can verify licence requirements before you book.

Where to set off in Tarragona

Port Tarraco, the main marina just west of the city centre, is the busiest departure hub with berths for yachts up to 30 metres. Nearby, Marina Port d'Abric in Cambrils offers a quieter alternative, and the Club Nàutic de Salou is ideal for day trips to the beaches of Vilafortuny. Further south, L'Ampolla and L'Ametlla de Mar provide access to the Cap de Creus nature reserve. Most charter companies also pick up from the Port de Reus and the small harbour at Altafulla, giving you a range of starting points depending on your planned route.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 58), best for a fast, luxurious day out with up to 12 guests; from €1,500 per day. For details, see this motor yacht listing on BoatHire24.
  2. Sailing catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42), stable and spacious, ideal for families or groups of up to 10; from €900 per day.
  3. Centre-console open boat (e.g. Axopar 28), perfect for exploring coves and snorkelling with 6‑8 guests; from €500 per half-day.
  4. Classic sloop (e.g. Sun Odyssey 410), a traditional sailing experience for 2‑6 guests, with skipper included; from €400 for a half-day.
  5. Large crewed yacht (e.g. Sunseeker Portofino 53), all‑inclusive luxury for up to 12 guests, with captain and host; from €2,500 per day.

Costs at a glance

  • Shared tour (small group): from €50 per person for a 2‑hour trip.
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €300 for a 6‑guest vessel.
  • Catamaran full-day: from €1,200 for up to 10 guests, with crew.
  • Luxury crewed yacht: from €2,500 for a full day, including food and drinks.

Seasons and licence information

The high season runs from June to September, when the Ponente wind can bring light afternoon breezes. May and October offer milder conditions with fewer crowds. Licences are generally required for boats over 15 hp, but you can always hire a skipper. For the most accurate and up-to-date requirements, BoatHire24 recommends confirming with the operator at the time of booking.

“If you're new to sailing, book a morning slot in June to enjoy flat calm seas before the mid-day wind picks up. For fishing, the waters off L'Ametlla de Mar are a local secret, try trolling for bonito in late summer.”

At a glance

  • Price band: €300, €2,500 per day
  • Peak months: June, September 2026
  • Trip length: half‑day (3‑4 hours) or full‑day (6‑8 hours)
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Tarragona

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Tarragona?

Yes, many operators offer boats under 15 hp that require no licence, and skippered charters are always available for larger vessels.

How far is it from Tarragona to the Ebro Delta?

The Ebro Delta Biosphere Reserve is about 20 nautical miles south of Port Tarraco, a one‑hour trip at cruising speed.

What is the best boat for a family day trip?

A Lagoon 42 catamaran provides stability, indoor and outdoor spaces, and plenty of room for children up to 10 guests.

Can I rent a boat for just a few hours?

Yes, half‑day rentals of three to four hours are common and start around €300 for a small motorboat.

BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare charters, read reviews and book with confidence. If you own a boat in Tarragona, you can browse the listings on BoatHire24 to see how to list your vessel and reach thousands of potential renters. The marketplace connects local owners with guests from around the world.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Tarragona and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
